supporting_evidence: During collapse, detail probes show interpolated channels changing every frame (`revealProgress` 0.904 -> 0.009 and `interpolatedOpacity` 0.853 -> 0.009) while render-geometry channels stay at collapsed target (`detailFrameHeightParam=0.000`, `detailVisibleHeight=0.000`, `detailOpacity=0.000`).

Animation lifecycle completes normally (`probe.collapse.end` followed by `table.afterHeightAnimation` and `animation.complete`), so animation infrastructure is active while detail-region geometry remains snapped.
contradicting_evidence: No evidence indicates tentative-vs-committed state promotion failure; symptoms are isolated to render-time geometry interpolation.
missing_evidence: The incident payload is capped at 50 breadcrumbs, and high-frequency detail probes consumed the budget before synchronized `geometryScopeProbe` events were exported.
One transition capture containing row/container, outer-card shell, border path, detail boundary, and below-boundary child probes in the same frame sequence.
candidates.next_discriminating_check: Run one collapse transition with selective probe emission (target row + collapse window + reduced sample cadence) and verify whether `geometryScopeProbe` survives export and identifies the earliest diverging scope.
